text:
Welcome to people joining this Slack who are interested in participating in GSoC.

Information about the R Project's involvement in GSoC is provided on this wiki: <https://github.com/rstats-gsoc/gsoc2026/wiki>. Please read the background information there before asking questions here. In particular, note:
• The main communication channel is currently the Google Group
• Projects are proposed in this table: <https://github.com/rstats-gsoc/gsoc2026/wiki/table-of-proposed-coding-projects> - they are added on a rolling basis, sometimes even quite close to the contributor deadline.
• There is advice on the wiki about preparing an application, which should be read alongside the general GSoC documentation.
This Slack is primarily for discussing contributions to base R (the code and documentation that is bundled in the source distribution of R) and to infrastructure that supports such contribution. There are sometimes one or two related GSoC projects - others relate to add-on (CRAN) packages and you will typically interact with maintainers of those packages through other channels (e.g. email/the package GitHub repo). This channel is to promote any projects related to base R and for basic announcements/reminders.

We welcome potential GSoC contributors to contribute to base R/contributor infrastructure, but please note we have limited capacity for chat and code review, so response times may be long! Good first issues may be promoted through the <#C0345T1S70A|getting-started> channel or you can attend an <http://We%20welcome%20potential%20GSoC%20contributors%20to%20contribute%20to%20base%20R/contributor%20infrastructure,%20but%20please%20note%20we%20have%20limited%20capacity%20for%20chat%20and%20code%20review,%20so%20response%20times%20may%20be%20long!%20Good%20first%20issues%20may%20be%20promoted%20through%20the%20#getting-started%20channel%20or%20you%20can%20attend%20an%20[Office%20Hour](https://contributor.r-project.org/events/office-hours/|Office Hour> for help finding a task that suits your skills.
